summary: Russian forces intensified strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure, including a FAB‑250 attack on Kramatorsk and rocket fire on Derhachi causing civilian casualties. Ukraine reported a high threat of mass missile attacks on Kyiv and noted Russian use of the medium‑range Oreshnik missile near Avdiivka. Diplomatic tensions rose as Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya arrived in Kyiv.
key_points: Russian air forces launched over 300 missiles targeting Kyiv defense industry facilities, as reported by the Russian Foreign Ministry (Source: @tass_agency/377511).

Five FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two and damaging 14 multi‑storey buildings, an educational institution, administrative structures and a postal office (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
A Russian rocket strike on Derhachi injured 19 people, killed a 68‑year‑old man, and hit a civilian enterprise in Kharkiv region (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Russia claimed its Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missile struck a Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).

Ukraine warned of a high threat of a mass missile strike on Kyiv and Kyiv oblast within three days, listing potential weapons including X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr and Kinzhal missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya arrived in Kyiv, indicating increased political activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30403).
Russia announced systematic strikes on Ukrainian defense industry enterprises in Kyiv, following an earlier strike on Starobelsk (Source: @tass_agency/377480).

sections.executive_summary: Russian air forces launched extensive missile strikes on Kyiv's defense industry, while a FAB‑250 bombing hit Kramatorsk causing civilian damage. Ukraine warned of imminent mass missile attacks on the capital, citing a range of advanced weapons. Diplomatic tensions rose with the arrival of Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya in Kyiv.

points: A rocket attack on Derhachi in Kharkiv region injured 19 civilians, killed a 68‑year‑old man, and struck a civilian enterprise, highlighting ongoing threats to populated areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).

Ukraine's defense ministry warned of a high probability of a mass missile strike on Kyiv and Kyiv oblast within three days, listing specific missile types that could be used (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).

Ukrainian authorities reported that a 18‑year‑old was arrested near a Ministry of Defense facility in Kyiv for gathering location data on military movements during the May 24 mass attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81454).
Ukraine noted that Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs over Kharkiv region, indicating continued use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/62725).
The Russian Foreign Ministry announced systematic strikes on Ukrainian defense industry enterprises in Kyiv, following earlier attacks on Starobelsk (Source: @tass_agency/377480).
Russia claimed its Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missile struck a temporarily Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Russia reported that a gas tanker "Arktik Metagaz" was damaged near Malta in March, accusing Ukraine of the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81452).
Russian officials warned foreigners to leave Kyiv and stated that Russian forces are beginning consistent strikes against Ukrainian defense industry facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russian forces used FAB‑250 bombs on Kramatorsk, causing civilian injuries and extensive building damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
A Russian missile strike hit Derhachi in Kharkiv region, resulting in civilian casualties and damage to a local enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs over Kharkiv oblast, indicating intensified air operations in the east (Source: @kpszsu/62725).
Ukrainian reports indicated the presence of Oreshnik missile impacts near Avdiivka, a contested area in the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
No specific northern front operational details were present in the source messages.
No specific southern front operational details were present in the source messages.
Russian air forces launched over 300 missiles targeting Kyiv's defense industry facilities (Source: @tass_agency/377511).
Ukrainian warnings cite potential use of X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr and Kinzhal missiles against Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes over Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62725).
Reports of new reactive UAVs heading toward Sumy and Poltava regions suggest expanded drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93641).

key_developments: Russian air forces launched over 300 missiles targeting Kyiv defense industry facilities, indicating a large‑scale aerial campaign (Source: @tass_agency/377511).
Five FAB‑250 bombs struck Kramatorsk, injuring at least two and damaging multiple civilian structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
A rocket strike on Derhachi injured 19 civilians and killed a 68‑year‑old man, showing continued missile threats to populated areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Ukraine warned of a high threat of a mass missile strike on Kyiv within three days, listing specific advanced missile types (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Russia reported that its Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missile hit a Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya arrived in Kyiv, reflecting heightened political activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30403).
Russia announced systematic strikes on Ukrainian defense industry enterprises in Kyiv, following earlier attacks on Starobelsk (Source: @tass_agency/377480).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and bomb strikes continue to target Ukrainian civilian and industrial infrastructure, with recent FAB‑250 attacks and rocket fire causing casualties. Ukraine faces an elevated risk of a coordinated mass missile barrage on Kyiv, potentially employing a range of advanced weapons. The use of Oreshnik missiles near Avdiivka suggests Russia is expanding its medium‑range strike capabilities, increasing pressure on eastern front positions.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has intensified civil defense measures, arresting individuals suspected of aiding Russian targeting and warning civilians to avoid potential strike zones. Internationally, the arrival of Belarusian opposition leader Tikhanovskaya in Kyiv underscores growing political support for Ukraine. Russia maintains its narrative of Ukrainian aggression, accusing Kyiv of attacks on its assets such as the gas tanker near Malta.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch sites, especially those capable of deploying X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr and Kinzhal missiles, is critical for early warning. UAV activity reported in Sumy and Poltava regions may indicate reconnaissance or strike preparation. The arrest of a youth involved in mapping Russian strike locations highlights Russian exploitation of local collaborators.
